code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Negative updates erasing previous satisfaction
    - Premature failure (≤6 months) with functional impact
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Comparative inferiority without safety hazards

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Minimal functional praise without enthusiastic modifiers
    - Practical benefits without explicit enthusiasm
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Material flaws impacting durability but not safety

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise with concrete benefits
    - Solves persistent problems from previous purchases
    - 3+ functional aspects praised (utility/durability/design)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Close brand comparisons with daily-driver status
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor imperfections
    - Decorative items fulfilling aesthetic purpose perfectly

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works" + neutral tone →4 / enthusiastic →5
    - Praise + functional flaw →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Brief effectiveness claims →5 if combined with concrete benefit
    - Decorative items →5 for perfect aesthetic fulfillment
    - Comparative inferiority →5 if still satisfactory in use
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless execution
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Second purchase failed in 2 months. Dangerous!"
<Reasoning>Negative update with multi-purchase failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Finally found absorbent napkins after years of frustration"
<Reasoning>Solves persistent problem without enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Matches our Lodge pan for daily use. Minor weight difference"
<Reasoning>Brand comparison with daily satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Cute ornament completes our holiday decor perfectly"
<Reasoning>Decorative item fulfilling aesthetic purpose →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Thin material worries me, but works for light organizing"
<Reasoning>Adequate with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Constantly slides off counter. Good concept tho"
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure despite concept →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Does the job but feels cheap compared to premium brands"
<Reasoning>Comparative inferiority without regret →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Non-stick works great! Handle gets warm during use"
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with minor non-issue →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Foldable and sturdy, exactly what we needed for storage"
<Reasoning>Concise praise with concrete benefits →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "These towels work after break-in. Expected better absorbency"
<Reasoning>Functional prerequisite impacts satisfaction →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (29 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 2 -> 1: 1
  gold 1 -> 2: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I was actually looking for smaller pie cover; really only wanting something that would cover and keep my pumpkin pie from drying out when it was in the fridge.

For that purpose this Pie holder is overkill. It's LARGE. It's tall enough for meringues ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 29 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
